Details: A full-scale study observed initial removal in GAC contactors of 60 percent of TOC and 70 to 77 percent of DOC. Removal declined over time. After 40,000 bed volumes treated, removal decreased to 40 percent of TOC and 26 percent of DOC for coal-based GAC and 20 percent of TOC and 8 percent of DOC for peat GAC [2469]. Full-scale and bench-scale studies of contactors designed to be biological activated carbon filters also showed high initial removals (87 to 90 percent), which the authors attributed to GAC adsorption prior to the development of biological activity [2446, 2447].
